winning_move_cell_label: {hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Which labeled empty cell lets {query_player} win immediately?
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} {query_player} will play one labeled empty cell. Which label completes a winning connection?
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Find the labeled empty cell where {query_player} can move to win now.
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Which candidate cell gives {query_player} a connected path between the required sides?
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Choose the one labeled cell that makes an immediate winning chain for {query_player}.
connection_gap_count: {hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} What is the minimum number of empty cells {query_player} must fill to connect the required sides?
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Count the fewest empty cells needed for {query_player} to complete a side-to-side connection.
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} How many empty cells are needed on a shortest connecting route for {query_player}?
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Determine the minimum connection gap for {query_player}.
{hex_rule_text} {red_goal_text} {blue_goal_text} Find the smallest number of empty cells that would complete {query_player}'s connection.
candidate_neighbor_count: {hex_rule_text} The green cell is the reference cell. How many adjacent cells are {neighbor_state}?
{hex_rule_text} Count the {neighbor_state} neighbors touching the green reference cell.
{hex_rule_text} Around the green cell, how many neighboring cells are {neighbor_state}?
{hex_rule_text} How many cells adjacent to the green cell are {neighbor_state}?
{hex_rule_text} Use the six cells touching the green reference cell. How many of them are {neighbor_state}?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_open_board: a Hex board with red and blue stones, colored goal sides, and open cells
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_crowded_board: a Hex board with red and blue stones, colored goal sides, and a crowded mix of open cells and stones
code_prompt_defaults.hex_rule_text: In Hex, adjacent cells are the six cells sharing an edge.
code_prompt_defaults.red_goal_text: Red connects the left and right red sides.
code_prompt_defaults.blue_goal_text: Blue connects the top and bottom blue sides.
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_winning_move_cell_label: set "annotation" to one pixel-space point [x, y] at the center of the chosen winning cell
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_winning_move_cell_label: set "answer" to the single candidate letter of the empty cell that lets {query_player} win immediately
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_connection_gap_count: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space points [x, y] at the centers of the empty cells in the unique minimum connection gap for {query_player}
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_connection_gap_count: set "answer" to the minimum number of empty cells {query_player} must fill to connect the required sides
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_candidate_neighbor_count: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space points [x, y] at the centers of {neighbor_state} neighboring cells adjacent to the green reference cell; use [] if none match
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_candidate_neighbor_count: set "answer" to the number of {neighbor_state} neighboring cells adjacent to the green reference cell
